commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
These. All is omitted, though it is found chap. viii. 38, and in manuscript ii. (Kennicott)
These remarks, which may appear to some minute, show that all Hebrew manuscripts are not absolutely alike, as some once pretended; and that the Hebrew Bible is not printed from the best copies. (Haydock)
"The sacred volume in Hebrew has, therefore, been published with such carelessness, as no other printed copy with which I am acquainted. "(Houbigant, prol. p. 2.)
Yet this is the copy, which alone Protestants hold up as the standard of perfection, and the Rule of Faith! (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
